agency: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
local_agency: NY Office of Temporary and Disability Assistance (OTDA) / NYC HRA
description: Helps low-income households pay heating and cooling costs. Includes Regular HEAP, Emergency HEAP, and Cooling Assistance.

eligibility.income_note: NY sets HEAP income limits at approximately 60% of State Median Income, which is roughly equivalent to 150% FPL for most household sizes.
eligibility.auto_eligible: Automatically eligible if receiving SNAP, SSI, or Temporary Assistance (TANF).
eligibility.program_period_note: Regular HEAP typically opens November 1. Emergency HEAP typically opens January. Cooling Assistance available in summer. Dates vary each year.

apply_in_person: Visit your local HRA office or call 212-331-3126.
how_to_apply: Apply online through ACCESS HRA, by phone, or at your local HRA office. Bring proof of income, utility bill, and ID.
citation: 42 USC 8621-8630 (federal LIHEAP); NY Social Services Law § 97
